DIRECI DIAL NUMBER
(2O2) A87-8739
after Jul-y 6--
(714) 25t-75o0
Jeevan s. Ahuja, Esq.
Counsel , Legal- Division
California Fair Political- Practices
cornmiss ion
428 J Street
Suite 800
P. O. Box 807
sacramento, california 
95804-08o7
Rer Request for Infornal Assistance
Dear Mr. Ahuj a:
As we have discussed on the telephone, this is a
request for infornal writt-en assistance from the Fair PoIitical
Practices Commission under Regulation 18329 (c) .1/ 
We have been
authorized to request this assistance by a cl-ient whose
obligations under the Political Reform Act, Government Code
S 8l-000 et seq. (the "Act"), are in question.
QUESTION
Does a person who provides property tax consulting
services under contract tc, a land developer or other parties
(but not a government agency) in connection with the
establishrnent of a local bond financing district 
come within
the definition of a ,,consultant,. under Regulation 18700(a) (2)?
PROPOSED CONCLUSION
A person who has not entered into a contract with a
state or local government agency to provide information,
advice, recommendation or counsel to the governnent agency does
L/
of
A1I references to
the California Coderegulations are to Title 2, Division 6
of Regulations.


MonnrsoN &Fornsrrn
Jeevan s. Ahuja, Esq.
June 26, 1990
Page Two
not come within the definition of a "consultant" under
Regulation 18700(a) (2), even if the person provides inforrnation
or advice to a land developer or other parties (other than a
government agency) in connection with the establishment of a
Iocal" bond financing district.
ANALYSIS
chapter 7 of the Act imposes certain conflict of
interest restrictions on t'public officials" 
of state and loca1
governments. cal,. Govtt. code S 87100 et seq.
Regulation 1870o defines ,a public official 
to include, among
others, a ,'consultant of ia state or local governnent agency."
The regulation defines a consultant as:
any natural person who provides,
contract, inf orrniation, advice,
recommendation o.r counsel to a state or
1ocal governnent agency, - . . .
Regulation S l-8700 (a) (2) (e phasis added).2/
The definition of a consultant thus provides that a
consultant be a natural person who provides certain services
to a state or loca] government agency under contract with
the agency.
The Mel1o-Roos Community Facilities Act of L982 |
Government Code S 53311 et seq., provides for the
establishment of special bond financing districts 
known as
comrnunity facilities 
districts 
("cFDt'). Establishnent of a
CFD enabl-es a 1ocal qovernnent agency to raise funds through
the sale of bonds, the proceeds of which are used to finance
the construction of community facilities 
(for example, fire
stations, libraries, 
schools and parks) within the area
covered by the CFD. Id. SS 53313, 53313.5. The bonds are
repaid by special taxes (,,CFD taxes,,) levied on the real
property located within the CFD. Id. S 53358.
Under the provisions of the Government Code, a CFD
is establ,ished by the action of the relevant focal
government agency. Id. S 53318 et seq. However, the
process of establishing a cFD typica]Iy is initiated by a
Iand developer or other pr:ivate party who makes a proposal
?/ The regufation contains
definition of a consultant:
under
certain exclusions from the
not relevant to this discussion.


MonnrsoN & Fornsrrn
Jeevan S. Ahuja, Esq.
June 26, 1990
Page Three
to the relevant government agency to establish a cFD to
finance the construction of facilities 
in an area the
developer owns and wishes to develop. The developerts
proposal to the government agency typicalJ.y includes a
description of the facilities 
to be constructed and an
estirnate of the total costs of the construction. 
The
developer,s presentation also rnay include a proposed "tax
spread,rr that is, a nethodology for apportioning the CFD
taxes over the parcel-s of real- property in the cFD.a/ If
the government agency wishes to proceed with the CFD, the
government agency and its counsel work with the developer,
bond counsel,, a bond underwriter and others to complete the
plans for the cFD. once compl-eted and approved, the cFD
proposal must be approved in a special election before the
CFD can finally be establ j-shed. Id. 53325.3.
our client is a special tax consultant vrho designs
the tax spread methodology and perforns the calculations
required in connection with a proposed cFD. (This is
accomplished by use of special computer software which our
client has developed.) This 1etter addresses cases in which
our client provides its services under contract with the
land developer or other private party (but not the
government agency) in connection with a proposed cFD. In
these cases, our cl.ient has no contractr written or oraL,
rrrith the government agency .
As discussed above, the definition of a consultant
under Regulation 18700(a) (2) provides that a consultant is a
person that provides certain services under contract with a
government agency. Because our client, under the facts set
forth above, has no contract with the government agency in
connection with his CFD work, he is not a consultant under
Regulation S l-8700 (a) (2).
1/ 
CFD taxes typically are apportioned based on the
the parcel , the size of the structure to be built on
parcel, the density of the proposed development, the
increased traffic 
burden the developnent will cause
factors.
size of
the
or other


MonnrsoN & Fonnsrnn
Jeevan s. Ahuja, Esq.
June 26, 199 o
Page Four
when you have received this letter, kindly stamp or
nark the encLosed copy as "received" and return the copy to
me in the enclosed stamped and self-addressed envelope for
our fiIes.
Thank you for your consideration of this rnatter.
should you have any questions, please do not hesitate to
telephone ne.

ATToRNEYS AT LAW
19900 M^CAKTHUR BOULEVARD
IRVINE, CALIFORNIA 9215
TELEPHONE (774) 2s7-7500
TELEFACSIMILE (774) 2s1-O9O0
NEW YORK
WASHINGTON, D.C
LONDON
HONG KONG
TOKYO
September 26, 1990
VIA FEDERAT EXPRESS
Jeevan S. Ahuja, Esg.
Counsel, Legal Division
California Fair Political
Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95804-0807
Re: Supplement to Request For
Informal Assistance
Dear Mr. Ahuja:
Pursuant to our telephone conversation on August
29, this letter is to supplement our request for informal
written assistance dated June 26, 1990.
As discussed more fully in our June 26 letter, it
is our position that a person who has not entered into a
contract with a state or local government agency to provide
information, advice, recommendation or counsel to the
government agency does not come within the definition of a
,,consultant,, under Regulation 18700(a)(2).L/ In our
telephone conversation, you asked us to provide more
information about the relationship our client has with the
public agency in connection with a proposed community
f acilities 
district 
(,,CFD,, ) . 
You explained that the Fair
PoliticaI Practices Commission (,,FPPCr,) staff had decided to
conduct a facts and circumstances review before rendering
advice on consultant conflicts of interest questions. This
was at least in part because of a recent situation in which
a city contracted with a land developer for the developer to
provide two staff members to the city to work on a land use
l/of
A11
the references to regulations are to Title Z, Division 6
California Code of Regulations.


Monnrsoll &Fornsrrn
Jeevan s. Ahuja, Esq.
september 26, L99o
Page Two
application submitted by the developer. You expfained that
although the t$/o staff rnernbers were not under contract to
the city, the FPPC staff had taken the position that, on the
facts and circumstances of the case, the staff persons were
,,consu1tants,, under Regulation 18700 (who thus coufd not
continue to receive income fron the developer while working
for the city on the developer,s application).
In the case you described, the two staff mernbers,
although not under an express contract to the city, clearly
were providing services to the city and served in the role
of city staff. 
Conflict of interest concerns may well be
raised where city staff members (or their functional
equivalent) are on the payrolL of a private party whose land
use application is being handled by those staff rnembers.
our client's situation, however, is conpletely
distinguishable .
As discussed in our June 26 letterr our client is a
special tax consultant who designs the tax spread
methodology and performs the calculations for CFDs. Our
Ietter addressed the cases in which our client provides
services under contract with the rel-evant developer or other
private party, such as bond counsel (but not the government
agency) (,,contract Party., ), in connection with a proposed
CFD. Our client has no contract, written or oral, express
or irnplied, with the governnent agency. Moreover, our
client does not act as the functional equivalent of the
public agencyrs staff. 
Our client works exclusively for the
Contract Party and subrnits its work product only to the
Contract Party. Once our client has subnitted its \4rork
product to the Contract Party, the r{ork product becornes the
property of the Contract Party, and our client has no
further control over its disposition. 
The Contract Party
generally turns the work product over to the public agency
as part of the CFD proposal. The Contract Party may ask our
client to attend meetings with the contract Party and the
public agency to answer the public agency,s questions in
regard to the CFD proposal, but our client does not deal"
with the public agency other than as a contractor to the
Contract Party.
In sumr our client has no wrj-tten or oral
contractual arrangement with the public agency, functions
independently of the public agency and does not serve as the
functional equivalent of public agency staff. 
The public
agency is aware at alL times that our client is working for
a private party and not the public agency. on these ficts


MonnrsoN & Foensrrn
Jeevan S. Ahuja, Esq.
september 26, L99o
Page Three
and circumstances and for the additional reasons discussed
in our June 26 letter, 
we submit that our client is not a
consul-tant under Regulation 18700.
As always, please call me should you have any
further questions or comments. Thank you for your
consideration.
RKO/fh
Robert K. olsen
u416 6 O 115652 )2
verl truly yours | ,/r
SrleK LU"-
